seed_rate.notes: Lower rate (2.5-3.5 kg/ha) is for Bt Cotton hybrids. Desi varieties require 12-15 kg/ha.

sowing.soil_conditions: Well-drained deep loamy soil. Seed bed should be moist but not waterlogged.

irrigation.notes: Delaying first irrigation to 30-40 days forces the plant roots to grow deeper.
Flowering squares begin to appear. Moisture stress at this stage leads to heavy square shedding.
Peak water demand stage. Adequate moisture ensures maximum boll setting.
Ensures proper fiber length, strength, and weight of cotton bolls.
Irrigation must be stopped to allow bolls to dry and burst open without catching rot or staining fiber.

fertilizer.method: Band placement below the seed line
fertilizer.notes: Apply entire dose of Phosphorus, 50% of Potassium, and 30% of Nitrogen at the time of sowing.

fertilizer.method: Side dressing followed by hoeing and irrigation
fertilizer.notes: Apply 35% Nitrogen and remaining 50% Potassium during square initiation (40-45 DAS).
Apply remaining 35% Nitrogen at flowering initiation (70-80 DAS) to prevent nitrogen depletion.

maturity_indicators: Cotton bolls burst open naturally exposing dry white fluffy lint
Inner boll walls become brown and dry
harvest.harvest_method: Manual hand picking in 3 to 4 sequential rounds (pickings)
harvest.notes: Pick clean cotton from fully burst bolls in morning hours (after dew dries) to maintain high fiber quality. Avoid leaf and trash contamination.
